title: Making carrageenan size for marbling
subtitle: Mixing, resting, and conditioning the seaweed-gel base for a marbling bath
excerpt: Carrageenan size, a gel made from dried seaweed powder dissolved in water, is the foundation of Western marbling. The size thickens the water just enough to support the paint on the surface while allowing it to be shaped with a comb or stylus. Getting the consistency right is the single most important variable in marbling: too thin and the paint sinks; too thick and the combed pattern will not move freely.
sourceNotes: The use of carrageenan as a marbling size was introduced in the mid-twentieth century as a replacement for gum tragacanth. Charles Woolnough, The Whole Art of Marbling (1853, 1881) describes earlier size formulations. Modern carrageenan technique is described in contemporary craft marbling publications following the same fundamental principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: A thickened water medium for marbling made from carrageenan powder (extracted from Chondrus crispus or Gigartina seaweed) dissolved in water at approximately 1 to 2 tablespoons per litre. The gel supports floating paints and allows them to be combed into patterns before transfer to paper.

The period, usually overnight, during which freshly blended carrageenan size is left undisturbed so that air bubbles introduced during blending can disperse. Bubbles in the size disrupt the paint surface during marbling.
Dragging a strip of newsprint across the surface of the size before each print to remove any residual paint from the previous print. Each print should begin with a clean size surface.

content.text: is prepared a day before it is needed. The

is not optional, a freshly blended size is full of microscopic air bubbles that pop on the surface during marbling and leave circular clear patches in the pattern. Preparing the size the evening before guarantees a bubble-free surface for the morning session.

items.name: Carrageenan powder
items.qty: 1 to 2 tablespoons per litre of water

items.name: Clean water, room temperature
items.qty: as needed to fill the tray to 3 to 4 cm depth

content.text: Measure the water into a blender. Add the carrageenan powder gradually while blending on medium speed, adding all the powder at once causes clumping. Blend for 2 minutes until there are no visible lumps.
Pour the blended size into the marbling tray and leave it overnight at room temperature. Do not refrigerate, cold size is too thick to work well.

Test the consistency before the session: drop a single drop of prepared marbling paint onto the surface. It should spread into a disc of about 4 to 8 cm. If it spreads to nothing (too thin), add a little more carrageenan to a fresh batch. If it barely spreads at all (too thick), dilute with water.

After lifting each print, drag a strip of newsprint across the full length of the tray surface to skim off residual paint. The skim strip picks up the leftover colour and reveals a fresh size surface for the next print. Do not skip this step, printing on contaminated size produces muddy colour mixes.

Carrageenan size keeps for 3 to 5 days at room temperature. It will develop a slight odour as it begins to ferment; add a few drops of household bleach (1 ml per litre) to extend the life if needed. Discard and replace when the surface begins to look slightly cloudy or when dropped paint no longer spreads evenly.

items.symptom: Surface is covered in small circles where bubbles have popped
items.cause: Size was used immediately after blending without resting overnight.
items.fix: Leave the size overnight before use. Skim the surface with newsprint once to remove visible bubbles before starting to marble.

items.symptom: Paint sinks rather than spreading on the size
items.cause: Size is too thick; or paint is too heavy.
items.fix: Thin the size by adding water and mixing. Thin the paint by adding distilled water, one drop at a time.
